text.title: Role-Specific 30-60-90 Plan Examples
text.summary: Concrete milestone examples for software engineer and sales representative roles, demonstrating how the Learn/Contribute/Execute framework adapts to different delivery cycles.

rule.statement: Engineering 30-60-90 plans must anchor milestones to code artifacts: merged PR, deployed feature, published doc, completed code review — not "learned the codebase".
rule.rationale: Learning the codebase is a means, not a milestone. The day-30 deliverable should be a first commit, however small, that demonstrates the environment is set up and the engineer can work in the team's workflow.

example.note: Software Engineer 30-day milestone: "Development environment fully set up, first small PR (bug fix or doc update) merged, all team workflows (CI/CD, code review, deployment) completed at least once, key stakeholders introduced."

Software Engineer 60-day milestone: "Owns and delivers a scoped feature independently — from technical design review through merge and monitoring for one week post-deploy. Participates in sprint planning and owns at least 2 code reviews per sprint."

Software Engineer 90-day milestone: "Recognized as a productive team member by the team (peer feedback). Owns a medium-complexity feature. Has contributed to at least one architectural discussion with a documented position. Leads or presents in one team sync."

rule.statement: Sales 30-60-90 plans must anchor to pipeline metrics, not activity counts; "made 50 calls" is not a milestone — "built a qualified pipeline of $X" is.
rule.rationale: Activity counts measure effort, not output. A sales representative can make 100 calls to unqualified prospects and have zero qualified pipeline. Pipeline value and deal stage distribution are the leading indicators of ramp performance.

example.note: Sales Representative 30-day milestone: "Product certified (passed internal product test), completed 5 ride-along calls with senior reps, built first territory plan, run first 3 discovery calls independently."

Sales Representative 60-day milestone: "50% of full-quota pipeline built in the CRM with stage-correct opportunities. Has presented at least 5 demos. Has submitted at least 2 proposals. Has developed a territory account map."

Sales Representative 90-day milestone: "Hit ramp quota (50-75% of full quota). Has closed at least 1 deal independently. Manages full sales cycle without manager shadowing. Has a territory strategy for Q2 documented."

antipattern.description: Using the same plan template for engineering and sales roles without role-specific adaptation.
antipattern.reason: A Sales 60-day milestone (50% pipeline) is fundamentally unlike an engineering one (feature delivered). Generic templates produce milestones that don't match either role's actual delivery cycle, making the review a formality.

antipattern.description: Setting stretch goals in the Learn phase (days 1-30) to signal high expectations.
antipattern.reason: Stretch goals in the first 30 days reward speed over quality, incentivize the hire to skip context-building, and produce poor output that creates downstream debt. The manager signals expectations through the day-60 and day-90 milestones, not by front-loading delivery.

antipattern.description: Plans written solely by HR using a generic template, then handed to the hire on Day 1 as a done document.
antipattern.reason: Plans the hire did not co-author become external checklists with no ownership. Drift starts in week 2; by day 30, nobody references them. Thirty minutes of co-authoring on Day 1 produces a plan the hire treats as a personal commitment.
